No Postman? No Problem — Testing APIs with curl
Sometimes you can't use Postman to test an API and you can't install other tools — maybe you're SSH'd into a server (servers usually have no GUI). That's when you reach for curl.
You Can Understand APIs by Buying Lottery Tickets — A Real-Life Walkthrough of RESTful APIs
Let's start with a simple lottery example and step-by-step unpack the mystery of APIs, REST, and RESTful APIs.
Build Your First LINE Bot with Node.js — Using OpenAI GPT-3 and GPT-3.5 as Examples

OpenAI's ChatGPT has been blowing up recently, and OpenAI also exposes an API we can integrate with. This post focuses on GPT-3 but also covers how to switch to ChatGPT API. We'll use Node.js to build a LINE chatbot backed by OpenAI.
Use Google Apps Script + Google Sheets to Spin Up a Quick API

Sometimes you just need a quick API and don't want to stand up a whole backend (Koa, Express, Laravel — too much for a one-off). Google Apps Script + Google Sheets gets you there fast — sheet as the database, GAS as the lightweight server. This post walks through creating the sheet, reading rows, exposing them as a GET endpoint, deploying as a Web App, and formatting the response as clean JSON.
What's an API? And What's a RESTful API?

RESTful API is a phrase you hear constantly as a frontend dev. But what's actually behind it? This post walks through what an API is (with a postman + painter analogy), then builds up to RESTful API as a design style — the conventions around plural vs. singular resource paths, HTTP methods, URL casing, versioning, and why using REST conventions makes your code easier for other devs to read.
